description: SIF Implementation Specification as a rich Neo4j graph — 159 object types, 15,620 fields, complex types, codesets, data types, and XML element tree. All nodes carry :SifModel label for multi-standard graph compatibility.
_note: No startup needed. Docker --restart unless-stopped keeps containers running. First-time init: sifSpecGraphManager -start --assetsDir=./assets

definition.name: sif_spec_list_objects
definition.description: List all 159 SIF object types. Returns name, tableName, field count for each.

definition.name: sif_spec_describe_object
definition.description: Describe a SIF object type: shows all fields with name, mandatory flag, data type, description, XPath, CEDS ID, and nesting depth. Fields are first-class graph nodes connected via HAS_FIELD.

name.description: SIF object name (e.g., StudentPersonal, AssessmentRegistration). Case-insensitive.

definition.name: sif_spec_describe_field
definition.description: Get full detail for a specific field by its XPath (the canonical SIF identifier). Returns type, codeset values, complex type membership, CEDS mapping.

definition.name: sif_spec_search_fields
definition.description: Search SIF fields by name or description substring across all objects. Useful for finding where a concept appears (e.g., 'birthDate', 'grade', 'ethnicity').

pattern.description: Substring to match against field names or descriptions (case-insensitive)

definition.name: sif_spec_fields_by_ceds_id
definition.description: Find all SIF fields that map to a specific CEDS Global ID. The bridge between SIF and CEDS standards.

cedsId.description: CEDS Global ID (e.g., 000040 for City, 000267 for StateProvince)

definition.name: sif_spec_inbound_references
definition.description: Find which SIF objects reference a given object via RefId foreign keys. Answers: 'What objects point to StudentPersonal?'

definition.name: sif_spec_outbound_references
definition.description: Find which SIF objects a given object references via RefId foreign keys. Answers: 'What does AssessmentRegistration point to?'

definition.name: sif_spec_find_path
definition.description: Find the shortest referential path between two SIF objects. Shows the chain of objects and RefId properties connecting them.

definition.name: sif_spec_search_by_name
definition.description: Search SIF object names by substring match. Use when you don't know the exact object name.

pattern.description: Substring to match against SIF object names (e.g., 'student', 'assessment')

definition.name: sif_spec_describe_complex_type
definition.description: Describe a SIF complex type (shared structure like Address, SIF_Metadata, Demographics). Shows which objects use it, child types, and sample fields.

name.description: Complex type name (e.g., Address, SIF_Metadata, Demographics, PhoneNumber)

definition.name: sif_spec_list_complex_types
definition.description: List SIF complex types sorted by how many objects use them. Shows shared structures like Address (used by many objects) vs specialized ones.

definition.name: sif_spec_describe_codeset
definition.description: Show the allowed values (codeset) for a field. Lookup by XPath or field name.

name.description: Field name (returns first 10 matches if multiple fields share the name)

definition.name: sif_spec_xml_tree
definition.description: Show the XML element tree structure for a SIF object. Navigable hierarchy showing how fields nest within the XML schema. Control depth with --depth parameter.

definition.name: sif_spec_semantic_search
definition.description: Semantic search across all 15,620 SIF fields using vector embeddings. Finds fields by meaning, not just name match. Example queries: 'student birth date', 'school grade level', 'ethnic background'.

query.description: Natural language search query describing the field concept you're looking for

definition.name: sif_spec_raw_cypher
definition.description: Execute a raw Cypher query against the SIF graph. Node types: :SifObject, :SifField, :SifComplexType, :SifSimpleType, :SifPrimitiveType, :SifCodeset, :SifXmlElement (all also labeled :SifModel). Relationships: HAS_FIELD, REFERENCES, HAS_TYPE, CONSTRAINED_BY, MEMBER_OF, CONTAINS, USES_COMPLEX_TYPE, CHILD_ELEMENT, HAS_ROOT_ELEMENT, REALIZED_BY, TYPED_AS.
